question: Will Aryna Sabalenka be the 2026 Women’s Wimbledon Winner? Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome of the question found at https://polymarket.com/market/will-aryna-sabalenka-be-the-2026-womens-wimbledon-winner. Background: Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026. This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament. If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”. If the 2026 Wimbledon Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”. The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

evidences.title: 2026 Women's Wimbledon Odds: Aryna Sabalenka Leads, Serena Williams Returns
evidences.content: The odds board starts where the rankings do, with Aryna Sabalenka leading at a 23% implied win probability. The world No. 1 paces Tennis Abstract's grass-court Elo ratings, a meaningful edge at the only Grand Slam played on grass. She is the rightful favorite, but in the current state of the women's game, favorite is far from inevitable. [...] Now, nearly four years since her last Grand Slam singles match ended in the third round of the U.S. Open, Williams returns to Wimbledon as a wild card entry—officially, and otherwise. At 44 and unseeded, Williams is not supposed to win Wimbledon, or even loom over its second week. But logic can't quantify her aura. Her mere entry has electrified interest around the tournament, driving so much action on Polymarket that the women's Wimbledon market has eclipsed the men's by more than $4 million in trading volume. While traditional sportsbooks price Williams as a massive underdog, her odds on prediction markets are inflated for one simple reason: people want to see her win. [...] Advertisement Williams has +5500 odds to win Wimbledon on FanDuel Sportsbook, which implies just a 1.8% probability. Yet on Polymarket, she is being priced closer to...

evidences.title: Wimbledon 2026: Longest Match Predictions & Odds - Polymarket
evidences.content: Expected high temperatures and heat alerts for Wimbledon 2026 (June 29–July 12) are activating the tournament’s heat rule, permitting 10-minute breaks between sets when the wet-bulb globe temperature reaches 30.1°C or higher. This pacing factor, paired with grass-court conditions that reward big servers such as Jannik Sinner, Alexander Zverev, and Ben Shelton, could extend individual sets through prolonged service holds and tiebreaks. The final-set tiebreak at 12–12, in place since 2019, continues to cap marathon possibilities like the 2010 Isner-Mahut record, while new video review on show courts may streamline close calls. Serena Williams’ wild-card return adds interest to potential longer women’s matches, though best-of-five men’s encounters remain the primary candidates for extended [...] Expected high temperatures and heat alerts for Wimbledon 2026 (June 29–July 12) are activating the tournament’s heat rule, permitting 10-minute breaks between sets when the wet-bulb globe temperature reaches 30.1°C or higher. evidences.content: ### Women's Contenders ### Aryna Sabalenka The world No. 1 enters Wimbledon as one of the favorites despite recent struggles. Sabalenka's powerful serve and aggressive baseline game are tailor-made for grass courts, and she has reached the semifinals in each of her last three Wimbledon appearances. However, questions remain after several disappointing losses in major events this season and concerns about her ability to close out matches under pressure. Even so, her raw power gives her arguably the highest ceiling in the field. evidences.title: Ranking the 2026 Wimbledon top contenders
evidences.content: ## Elena Rybakina Tennis Abstract odds: 7.0% First potential top-10 opponent: No. 6 Amanda Anisimova or No. 9 Linda Noskova (quarterfinals) Rybakina keeps you guessing. The 2022 Wimbledon champion secured her second Slam title at this year's Australian Open and is an incredible 17-5 against top-10 opponents over the last year. But after starting 2026 with a sparkling 30-6 record, she enters Wimbledon having lost four of her past six matches. She was a French Open favorite but fell in the second round, and she's 1-2 on grass this year. Her peak form might be even better than Sabalenka's, but like Sabalenka she comes to the All England Club with more questions than answers. ## Mirra Andreeva Tennis Abstract odds: 2.3% First potential top-10 opponent: No. 10 Muchova (fourth round) [...] Like Sinner, Sabalenka is an obvious favorite with obvious question marks. The four-time Slam champion -- and three-time Wimbledon semifinalist -- is just 7-4 in her past 11 matches, and against both Diana Shnaider at the French Open and Jessica Pegula in Berlin, she got bageled, 6-0, in the deciding set. For all of her remarkable recent success, she has won only one of her past six Slams, and now d...

evidences.title: Wimbledon 2026: Preview, draws, order of play & how to watch | LTA
evidences.content: World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka heads to SW19 in search of her first Grand Slam title on the grass, having appeared in three previous semi-finals. The Indian Wells champion will face competition from the Australian Open title holder and 2022 Wimbledon winner Elena Rybakina, who’s been hot on her heels this season. Elsewhere, defending champion Iga Swiatek returns hoping to repeat last year’s feat, as well as her 2025 final opponent Amanda Anisimova, who’s hoping for a run of form on the grass following her HSBC Championships appearance. They’ll be joined by world No. 3 Coco Gauff, 2025 quarter-finalist Mirra Andreeva, rising star Victoria Mboko as well as former champions Marketa Vondrousova and Barbora Krejcikova. [...] ## Wimbledon order of play & schedule You can check out the provisional schedule below, and the latest order of play will be available on the Wimbledon website each day during the tournament. WImbledon schedule Wimbledon order of play ## Wimbledon live scores Follow all the Wimbledon live scores on our Score Centre. evidences.title: Way-too-early Grand Slam predictions for the rest of 2026 - ESPN
evidences.content: Sabalenka had largely been considered the one to beat at the All England Club last season, and she reached her third semifinals at the tournament before being stunned by Anisimova in a three-set thriller. Although she has never won a title on grass, and Wimbledon remains the only Slam where she is yet to reach the final, she remains the most consistent player at majors and has advanced to the semifinals or better at every Slam she has played since the US Open in 2022. [...] Grass remains the most unpredictable surface for the women, largely because of its incredibly short window on the annual calendar. With just three weeks separating the end of the French Open and the start of Wimbledon, most players play in only one or two lead-in tournaments on grass and rarely practice on the surface outside of that time. It can be tricky for many, and the adjustment from the slow clay to the much faster grass is a challenge. [...] Although certainly more of a long shot, don't count out Fritz either. The American stalwart made his first semifinal appearance at the All England Club last year after two previous quarterfinal runs. He reached the final at the 2024 US Open, but grass seems to sui...

The Championships begins with two days of Gentlemen's and Ladies' Singles matches before Gentlemen's Doubles start on Wednesday, Ladies' Doubles on Thursday, and Mixed Doubles on Friday. evidences.title: [PDF] wimbledon - compendium
evidences.content: Recent Wimbledons might suggest the ladies’ singles will be as open as ever this summer, but Aryna Sabalenka, semi-finalist in her last two appearances at the All England Club and world No.1 for most of the last year, will be hoping not to feature in any upsets. Going into Roland-Garros, the 27-year-old had made the quarter-finals or better in each of her previous nine Grand Slam appearances. Iga Swiatek, who was dislodged from the top of the world rankings last year by Sabalenka, has been dominant on clay in recent years, but has rarely found her best form at The Championships. Top seed for the last three years, the Pole lost in the third round in 2022 and 2024 and in the quarter-finals in 2023. [...] Of the British women, Emma Raducanu, Katie Boulter and Sonay Kartal all have reasons for optimism. Raducanu has made the second week at The Championships in two of her three appearances and has shown signs this year of a return to the form that saw her win the US Open four years ago. Boulter is a proven grass court performer who has won matches at The Championships in each of the last four years, while Kartal made her big breakthrough last summer when she reached the third round o...

evidences.title: Qualifying
evidences.content: Send a new verification code. Skip to main content Header Image 1 1. Tickets 2. Qualifying # Qualifying # Qualifying Image 2: Qualifying ## Information on The Championships Qualifying Competition 2026 What is Qualifying? Held one week prior to The Championships, the Wimbledon Qualifying Competition comprises gentlemen’s and ladies’ singles competitions to determine the final main draw places for The Championships 2026. To secure a main draw place, players compete in a knock-out tournament and must win every one of their matches across three rounds to earn one of the 16 Championships’ places in each of the gentlemen’s and ladies’ draws.
